channel.title: Web Writers’ Collective
channel.description: Recent content on Web Writers’ Collective

item.title: Editing guide
item.description:
This guide is intended for existing members of WWC who may wish to edit this website for the following reasons:

  1. to post a notice to the noticeboard
  2. to submit their contribution to Anthologies
  3. to update their author bio and website
  4. to help fix typos or make minor improvements across this website

Prerequisites

A basic knowledge of git, or at least navigating around Github, is expected. If you do not believe you can continue with this, please feel free to send your contributions, or editing requests via email. While we cannot promise to update the website right away, we will try our best to do so as quickly as possible. However, please continue to read this guide as it will tell you what sort of document you need to send our way.

item.title: Community guidelines
item.description:
Code of conduct

Since we are a new community, we don’t have a code of conduct on this site yet. With that said, we operate under IndieWeb Code of Conduct.

If you would like to raise a potential violation of the code of conduct, please contact James in the Signal group.

Anyone found in violation of the Code of Conduct will be removed from the group.

Talking about discussions

Writing can be an intimite topic: discussing things like writers block, ideas we’re developing, and much more can make us feel vulnerable.

item.title: HWC Writing Edition
item.description: Meet the community every two months and chat about all things writing on the web.

item.title: ‘Repeat that, repeat’ challenge
item.description: Ruben and James started a collaborative writing challenge that invites readers to share their thoughts on the poem ‘Repeat that, repeat’ by Gerard Manley Hopkins.

item.title: Express your interest in WWC
item.description: Find out more about joining our positive, constructive and inclusive community.

item.title: About our collective
item.description:
Who we are

Web Writers Collective (WWC) is a Signal group for people who write on the web to chat about the culture of writing on the web, and to connect with (and maybe even collaborate with) like-minded writers.

If you are interested in writing, or who actively write, on the web, you may like WWC.

What we do

You might join the group to talk about—

  • Writing projects you’re working on that you would like feedback on
  • The culture of writing on the web: what to share, how to share, who to share with
  • Working through a specific challenge, i.e. how to articulate an idea, finding inspiration, working through writer’s block.
  • Community events you’re participating in, i.e. the Bear Blog Carnival.

We especially welcome people who write fiction, stories, creative non-fiction, poetry, life writing, personal blog posts, and everything in between.
